query: Find 3 recent papers on LLM agents

router.query: Find 3 recent papers on LLM agents

classification.reasoning: The query involves searching for academic literature in a specific domain
subtasks: Review papers on LLM agent research

execution_plan.success_criteria: Found at least 3 relevant sources with URLs and summaries

subagent.output:
1. What I did

Searched recent arXiv submissions for “LLM agents” / “large language model agents”, sorted by submission date. I selected 3 highly relevant papers from 2026-05-08 covering agent evaluation, multi-agent behavior, and autonomous data-science agents.

2. Result / output

Source 1: AgentEscapeBench: Evaluating Out-of-Domain Tool-Grounded Reasoning in LLM Agents
URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.07926
PDF: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.07926
Published: 2026-05-08
Authors: Zhengkang Guo, Yiyang Li, Lin Qiu, Xiaohua Wang, Jingwen Xv, et al.
Summary: Introduces AgentEscapeBench, an escape-room-style benchmark for evaluating whether LLM agents can perform unfamiliar tool-grounded reasoning. Tasks require agents to infer and revise tool-use procedures, manage long-range dependencies, track hidden state, propagate intermediate results, and invoke real external functions. Relevant because it directly targets evaluation of LLM agents beyond familiar workflows and short-horizon tool use.

Source 2: The Memory Curse: How Expanded Recall Erodes Cooperative Intent in LLM Agents
URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.08060
PDF: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.08060
Published: 2026-05-08
Authors: Jiayuan Liu, Tianqin Li, Shiyi Du, Xin Luo, Haoxuan Zeng, et al.
Summary: Studies how larger accessible memory/context affects LLM agents in multi-agent social-dilemma settings. Across 7 LLMs and 4 games over 500 rounds, expanded recall degraded cooperation in 18 of 28 model-game settings. The authors call this the “memory curse” and argue that longer memory can erode forward-looking cooperative intent rather than simply improving agent capability.

Source 3: RelAgent: LLM Agents as Data Scientists for Relational Learning
URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.07840
PDF: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.07840
Published: 2026-05-08
Authors: Xingyue Huang, Louis Tichelman, Jinwoo Kim, Krzysztof Olejniczak, İsmail İlkan Ceylan, et al.
Summary: Presents RelAgent, an autonomous LLM-based data scientist for relat
subagent.command:
/home/exedev/.hermes/hermes-agent/venv/bin/hermes chat -q You are a specialized research subagent.

Your task: Find 3 recent papers on LLM agents

You have access to these tools: web_search, web_extract, arxiv
You must meet this success criteria: Found at least 3 relevant sources with URLs and summaries

Execute the task autonomously. Do not ask the user for clarification unless 
a dimension genuinely requires their input (credentials, funds, legal risk).

Report back with:
1. What you did (2-3 sentences)
2. The result/output (include URLs, file paths, or code snippets)
3. Whether success criteria were met (yes/no/partial)
4. Any blockers or next steps needed

Be concise but thorough. If research, include at least 3 sources with URLs.
If code, include the git diff or test results.
 -m kimi-coding --provider openrouter -Q --yolo --max-turns 8
